label: Conceptual Entity
definition: An organizational header for concepts representing mostly abstract entities.

label: Property or Attribute
definition: A distinguishing quality or prominent aspect of a person, object, action, process, or substance.

label: Experimental Organism Anatomical Concept
definition: A biological structure, fluid or other substance that is present in a non-human organism; this includes anatomic entities found in organisms used in research settings. However, this grouping excludes single molecular entities.

label: Gene
definition: A functional unit of heredity which occupies a specific position on a particular chromosome and serves as the template for a product that contributes to a phenotype or a biological function.

label: Recombinant Granulocyte Colony-Stimulating Factor
definition: A recombinant therapeutic agent which is chemically identical to or similar to the endogenous cytokine granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF).  Secreted by monocytes, macrophages and neutrophils and other cells after activation, G-CSF stimulates the proliferation and differentiation of hematopoietic progenitor cells committed to the neutrophil/ granulocyte lineage. (NCI04)

label: Retired Concept
definition: Concept has been retired, and should not be used except to deal with old data.

label: Abnormal Cell
definition: An abnormal human cell type which can occur in either disease states or disease models.

label: Diagnostic or Prognostic Factor
definition: A characteristic of an organism that contributes to the diagnosis or treatment of a disease or to the prediction of clinical outcomes.

label: Malignant Neoplasm
definition: A neoplasm composed of atypical neoplastic, often pleomorphic cells that invade other tissues. Malignant neoplasms often metastasize to distant anatomic sites and may recur after excision. The most common malignant neoplasms are carcinomas, Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin lymphomas, leukemias, melanomas, and sarcomas.

label: Recombinant Erythropoietin
definition: A recombinant therapeutic agent which is chemically identical to or similar to the endogenous glycoprotein erythropoietin (Epo).  Epo promotes the differentiation and maturation of hematopoietic progenitors into erythrocytes; is a mitogen and a chemoattractant for endothelial cells; stimulates activated and differentiated B-cells and enhances B-cell immunoglobulin production and proliferation; and is hypoxia-inducible. (NCI04)

label: Anatomic Structure, System, or Substance
definition: A human biological structure, fluid or other substance; excludes single molecular entities.

label: Drug, Food, Chemical or Biomedical Material
definition: An organic or inorganic substance, element, isotope, food or other materials derived from microorganism, plant, or animal sources. They can be used for the prevention, diagnosis, treatment or research of disease states, for industrial purposes, or for the promotion or maintenance of organism viability or health.

label: Activity
definition: An active process; excludes processes and mechanisms which fulfill biological functions.

label: Biochemical Pathway
definition: A series of biochemical reactions which occurs in a known sequence and can result in all or part of the initiation, progression or completion of a biological process or function.

label: Biological Process
definition: An activity occurring within an organism, between organisms or among organisms and the mechanisms underlying such events.

label: Gene Product
definition: A protein, RNA or a complex that contains proteins and/or RNA.

label: Nuclear Receptor
definition: Proteins that regulate transcription via interaction with chromatin and basal transcription machinery at the promoter regions of genes. Nuclear receptors are ligand-dependent transcription factors including steroids, retinoids, vitamin D, unliganded thyroid hormone, and orphan receptors (unknown ligands). Coregulators (coactivators or corepressors) are important in mediating these interactions and thereby modulate positive or negative receptor activity.

label: Recombinant Granulocyte-Macrophage Colony-Stimulating Factor
definition: A recombinant therapeutic agent which is chemically identical to or similar to the endogenous glycoprotein cytokine growth factor granulocyte-macrophage colony stimulating factor (GM-CSF). Therapeutic GM-CSF induces the clonal expansion and differentiation of progenitor blood cells and stimulates the cytotoxicity of monocytes and neutrophils, thereby restoring immune function and host antitumor immune responses and stimulating engraftment of transplanted bone marrow tissue. (NCI04)

label: Transmembrane Transport
definition: Any subcellular or molecular process involved in translocation of a biological molecule across a cell membrane from one site or compartment to another. (NCI)

label: Organism
definition: A living entity.

label: Recombinant Tumor Necrosis Factor-Alpha
definition: A recombinant therapeutic agent which is chemically identical to or similar to the endogenous cytokine tumor necrosis factor-alpha with antineoplastic properties.  Tumor necrosis factor-alpha binds to and activates "death receptors" on the cell surface, resulting in apoptosis and cell death by the p53-independent extrinsic pathway. This agent also disrupts tumor vascularization. (NCI04)

label: Manufactured Object
definition: A physical object created by hand or machine.

label: Molecular Abnormality
definition: A molecular or cytogenetic abnormality which occurs in either human disease states or disease models.

label: Experimental Organism Diagnosis
definition: A condition or observation that is relevant to disease states, models of disease or characterization of those conditions in a non-human organism; this includes conditions or observations in organisms used in research settings.

label: HLA Class I Histocompatibility Antigen, A-2 Alpha Chain
definition: HLA class I histocompatibility antigen, A-2 alpha chain (365 aa, ~41 kDa) is encoded by the human HLA-A gene. This protein is involved in antigen presentation.

label: Chemotherapy Regimen or Agent Combination
definition: A combination of multiple drugs used in standard or clinical trial treatments. The order, dosage or dosing interval of the individual ingredients may not be specified.

label: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, Not Otherwise Specified
definition: A term referring to a group of diffuse large B-cell lymphomas which are biologically heterogeneous. These lymphomas have a centroblastic, immunoblastic, or anaplastic morphology.

label: Disease, Disorder or Finding
definition: A condition that is relevant to human neoplasms and non-neoplastic disorders.  This includes observations, test results, history and other concepts relevant to the characterization of human pathologic conditions.

label: Fibroblast Growth Factor
definition: A family of structurally-related growth factors with high affinity for heparin. These proteins modulate cell proliferation, angiogenesis, wound healing, embryogenesis and paracrine signaling pathways. Fibroblast growth factors (FGF) stimulate signal transduction through binding to specific members of the fibroblast growth factor receptors (FGFR) family tyrosine kinases.

label: Lung Small Cell Carcinoma
definition: A highly aggressive carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the lung. It is characterized by the presence of malignant small cells and more than 10 mitoses per 2 mm2. Necrosis is frequently seen. Metastatic disease is usually present at the time of diagnosis.

label: Lymphoma
definition: A malignant (clonal) proliferation of B- lymphocytes or T- lymphocytes which involves the lymph nodes, bone marrow and/or extranodal sites. This category includes Non-Hodgkin lymphomas and Hodgkin lymphomas.

label: Malignant Esophageal Neoplasm
definition: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm involving the esophagus.

label: Neoplastic Medium-Sized B-Lymphocyte with Basophilic Cytoplasm
Ovarian Monodermal Teratoma
definition: A teratoma that arises from the ovary and is characterized by the presence of tissues derived exclusively from one embryonic germ cell layer.

label: Recombinant Interferon Gamma
definition: A recombinant therapeutic agent which is chemically identical to or similar to the endogenous lymphokine interferon gamma (IFN-gamma) with antineoplastic, immunoregulatory, and antiviral activities. Therapeutic IFN-gamma binds to and activates the cell-surface IFN-gamma receptor, stimulating antibody-dependent cytotoxicity and enhances natural killer cell attachment to tumor cells. This agent also activates caspases, thereby inducing apoptosis in malignant cells. (NCI04)

label: Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definition: A carcinoma arising from squamous epithelial cells.  Morphologically, it is characterized by the proliferation of atypical, often pleomorphic squamous cells.  Squamous cell carcinomas are graded by the degree of cellular differentiation as well, moderately, or poorly differentiated.  Well differentiated carcinomas are usually associated with keratin production and the presence of intercellular bridges between adjacent cells.  Representative examples are lung squamous cell carcinoma, skin squamous cell carcinoma, and cervical squamous cell carcinoma.

label: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia
definition: Leukemia with an acute onset, characterized by the presence of lymphoblasts in the bone marrow and the peripheral blood. It includes the acute B lymphoblastic leukemia and acute T lymphoblastic leukemia.

label: Atypical Teratoid/Rhabdoid Tumor
definition: An aggressive malignant embryonal neoplasm arising from the central nervous system. It is composed of cells with a large eccentric nucleus, prominent nucleolus, and abundant cytoplasm. Mutations of the SMARCB1 gene or very rarely SMARCA4 (BRG1) gene are present. The vast majority of cases occur in childhood. Symptoms include lethargy, vomiting, cranial nerve palsy, headache, and hemiplegia.

label: BCOR Gene
definition: This gene plays a role in the repression of gene transcription.

label: Bilateral Breast Carcinoma
definition: Carcinoma that affects both breasts in a simultaneous or non-simultaneous manner.

label: Breast Adenocarcinoma
definition: The most common histologic type of breast carcinoma.  Representative examples include invasive ductal carcinoma not otherwise specified, ductal carcinoma in situ, inflammatory carcinoma, secretory carcinoma, signet ring cell carcinoma, tubular carcinoma, invasive lobular carcinoma, and lobular carcinoma in situ.

label: Condyloma Acuminatum
definition: A sexually transmitted papillary growth caused by the human papillomavirus. It usually arises in the skin and mucous membranes of the perianal region and external genitalia.

label: EBV-Related T/NK-Cell Lymphoproliferative Disorder
definition: T/NK-cell lymphoproliferative disorder that is associated with Epstein-Barr virus.

label: Endometrial Carcinoma
definition: A malignant tumor arising from the epithelium that lines the cavity of the uterine body.  The vast majority of endometrial carcinomas are adenocarcinomas; squamous cell and adenosquamous carcinomas represent a minority of the cases.  Endometrioid adenocarcinoma is the most frequently seen variant of endometrial adenocarcinoma.  Uterine bleeding is an initial clinical sign.  The prognosis depends on the stage of the tumor, the depth of myometrial wall invasion, and the degree of differentiation.

label: Extraocular Sebaceous Carcinoma
definition: A rare variant of sebaceous carcinoma that does not affect the ocular region.

label: Glandular Cell Neoplasm
definition: A benign or malignant, primary or metastatic epithelial neoplasm affecting the glandular cells.

label: Glioblastoma
definition: The most malignant astrocytic tumor (WHO grade 4).  It is composed of poorly differentiated neoplastic astrocytes and is characterized by the presence of cellular polymorphism, nuclear atypia, brisk mitotic activity, vascular thrombosis, microvascular proliferation, and necrosis. It typically affects adults and is preferentially located in the cerebral hemispheres. (Adapted from WHO)

label: Hematopoietic Growth Factor
definition: Endogenous growth factors which promote the development of blood cells.

label: Hodgkin Lymphoma
definition: A lymphoma characterized by the presence of relatively few malignant cells called Reed-Sternberg cells and mononuclear Hodgkin cells that are admixed with nonneoplastic inflammatory cells. The malignant cells have a distinctive immunophenotype; they are positive for CD30 and CD15 and negative for CD3, CD20, and CD45.

label: Invasive Breast Carcinoma
definition: A carcinoma that infiltrates the breast parenchyma.  The vast majority are adenocarcinomas arising from the terminal ductal lobular unit (TDLU).  Often, the invasive adenocarcinoma co-exists with ductal or lobular carcinoma in situ.  It is the most common carcinoma affecting women.

label: Keratinizing Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definition: Squamous cell carcinomas with morphologically prominent production of keratin.

label: Kidney Carcinoma
definition: A carcinoma arising from the epithelium of the renal parenchyma or the renal pelvis.  The majority are renal cell carcinomas.  Kidney carcinomas usually affect middle aged and elderly adults.  Hematuria, abdominal pain, and a palpable mass are common symptoms.

label: Lipomatosis
definition: A neoplastic process characterized by diffuse overgrowth of mature adipose tissue.

label: Lymph Node Disorder
definition: A non-neoplastic or neoplastic disorder that affects the lymph nodes.

label: Medulloblastoma
definition: A malignant, invasive embryonal neoplasm arising from the cerebellum or posterior fossa. It occurs predominantly in children and has the tendency to metastasize via the cerebrospinal fluid pathways. Signs and symptoms include truncal ataxia, disturbed gait, lethargy, headache, and vomiting. There are four histologic variants: classic medulloblastoma, large cell/anaplastic medulloblastoma, desmoplastic/nodular medulloblastoma, and medulloblastoma with extensive nodularity.

label: Meningioma
definition: A generally slow growing tumor attached to the dura mater. It is composed of neoplastic meningothelial (arachnoidal) cells. It typically occurs in adults, often women and it has a wide range of histopathological appearances. Of the various subtypes, meningothelial, fibrous and transitional meningiomas are the most common. Most meningiomas are WHO grade 1 tumors, and some are WHO grade 2 or 3 tumors. Most subtypes share a common clinical behavior, although some subtypes are more likely to recur and follow a more aggressive clinical course. (Adapted from WHO)

label: Myeloid Neoplasm
definition: Proliferation of myeloid cells originating from a primitive stem cell.

label: Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma
definition: Distinct from Hodgkin lymphoma both morphologically and biologically, non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL) is characterized by the absence of Reed-Sternberg cells, can occur at any age, and usually presents as a localized or generalized lymphadenopathy associated with fever and weight loss. The clinical course varies according to the morphologic type. NHL is clinically classified as indolent, aggressive, or having a variable clinical course. NHL can be of B-or T-/NK-cell lineage.

label: Ovarian Sertoli-Leydig Cell Tumor
definition: A benign or malignant sex cord-stromal tumor arising from the ovary. It is characterized by the presence of neoplastic Leydig cells. Signs and symptoms include hirsutism, menorrhagia and metrorrhagia. It may be associated with trisomy 8.

label: Pineoblastoma
definition: A poorly differentiated malignant embryonal neoplasm arising from the pineal region.  It usually occurs in children and it is characterized by the presence of small immature neuroepithelial cells.  It may follow an aggressive clinical course.

label: Prostate Carcinoma
definition: One of the most common malignant tumors afflicting men.  The majority of carcinomas arise in the peripheral zone and a minority occur in the central or the transitional zone of the prostate gland.  Grossly, prostatic carcinomas appear as ill-defined yellow areas of discoloration in the prostate gland lobes.  Adenocarcinomas represent the overwhelming majority of prostatic carcinomas.  Prostatic-specific antigen (PSA) serum test is widely used as a screening test for the early detection of prostatic carcinoma.  Treatment options include radical prostatectomy, radiation therapy, androgen ablation and cryotherapy.  Watchful waiting or surveillance alone is an option for older patients with low-grade or low-stage disease.

label: Recombinant Human Stem Cell Factor
definition: A therapeutic glycoprotein cytokine chemically identical to or similar to endogenous human stem cell factor with hematopoietic activity. Recombinant human stem cell factor (rhSCF) binds to the receptor tyrosine kinase c-kit, which may stimulate the growth of peripheral blood progenitor cells (PBPCs). This agent works synergistically with other hematopoietic growth factors. rhSCF may promote bone marrow recovery after myeloablative therapies and procedures.

label: Uveal Melanoma
definition: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract.  It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe.  Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia.  Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound.  Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection.  Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation.  Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types.  The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

label: Access
definition: The right to obtain or make use of or take advantage of something (as services or membership); the right to enter.  Also, an act or means of approaching.

label: Accessory
definition: Something added to extend a whole and to make an addition.

label: Accommodations
definition: A temporary place to stay, such as a hotel or similar space. It is sometimes used to include the associated food and services.

label: Accomplishment
definition: Something that has been achieved or completed successfully.

label: Adjudication
definition: The final judgment in a legal proceeding.

label: Affiliation
definition: A formal association between entities.

label: Agent
definition: An active power or cause (as principle, substance, physical or biological factor, etc.) that produces a specific effect.

label: Aim
definition: The goal intended to be attained (and which is believed to be attainable).

label: Amplification
definition: Addition of extra material.

label: Analog
definition: A synthetic chemical which structurally or functionally resembles a naturally occurring compound.

label: Appreciation
definition: To be fully aware of the value of something; realize fully.

label: Approach
definition: A way of entering or leaving.

label: Artifact
definition: A structure or appearance that is not naturally present, but has been introduced though manipulation.

label: Assembly
definition: A group of parts that fit together to form a self-contained unit.

label: Assent
definition: Agreement with a statement or proposal to do something.

label: Asymmetry Index
definition: A normalized measure of a difference between 2 quantities, expressed as a percentage difference of either their mean or their sum, whichever is defined by a user.

label: Authority
definition: A person or group with the power or right to give orders or make decisions for a particular area of activity.

label: Award
definition: A prize given for achievement in a specific area, including monies given on the basis of merit.

label: Axis
definition: A straight line through a body or figure that satisfies certain conditions.

label: Background
definition: Existing conditions, especially those that would be confused with the phenomenon to be observed or measured.

label: Bad Debt
definition: Loans or outstanding balances owed that are no longer deemed recoverable and must be written off.

label: Baseline
definition: A starting point to which things may be compared.

label: Basis
definition: The fundamental assumptions underlying an explanation or a relation that provide the foundation for something.

label: Batch
definition: A quantity of people or things treated or regarded as a group, especially when subdivided from a larger group.

label: Benchmark
definition: A standard by which something can be measured or judged.

label: Benefit
definition: The positive effects of something.

label: Bias
definition: Any deviation of results or inferences from the truth, or processes leading to such deviation.

label: Blackout
definition: A suspension of service (especially of radio or tv broadcasting).

label: Border
definition: A boundary line or the area immediately inside the boundary.

label: Bulk
definition: Large size, volume, or amount.

label: Category
definition: A grouping of items based on some commonality or by user defined characteristics.

label: Cell
definition: Any small compartment.

label: Chain
definition: A series of objects linked, connected, or associated together in linear fashion (e.g., the assemblage of atoms, radicals and chemical groups in a chemical compound, or an assemblage of individual microorganisms).

label: Channel
definition: An independent acquisition scheme, i.e., a route or conduit through which flows data consisting of one particular measurement using one particular parameter.

label: Checkbox
definition: A space on a paper form or an on-screen graphical display that can be filled or selected to choose an option.

label: Clinical Generalizability
definition: The extent to which the findings of a clinical trial can be reliably extrapolated from the subjects who participated in the trial to a broader patient population and a broader range of clinical settings. (ICH)

label: Code
definition: A symbol or combination of symbols which is assigned to the members of a collection.

label: Computer Programming Object
definition: In object-oriented programming, an object is a self-contained entity that consists of both data and procedures to manipulate the data.

label: Conceptual Parent
definition: Something from which one or more similar and separate things have developed, or to which they are attached.

label: Concern
definition: A matter of interest or importance.

label: Conclusion
definition: A position or opinion or judgment reached after consideration.

label: Confidentiality
definition: Treatment of information so that it is not divulged in ways that are inconsistent with the understanding of the original disclosure. Particularly, the ethical principle or legal right that a physician or other health professional will hold secret all information relating to a patient, unless the patient gives consent permitting disclosure.

label: Consent
definition: Permission to do something.

label: Construct
definition: An abstract or general idea inferred or derived from specific instances.

label: Content
definition: Everything that is included in a collection, container, or communication.

label: Context
definition: The universe of discourse that surrounds a language unit and helps to determine its interpretation.

label: Convention
definition: Something regarded as a normative example.

label: Coordinate
definition: A number or other designation that identifies a position relative to an axis or grid.

label: Copy
definition: A secondary representation, reproduction, or replica.

label: Cost
definition: The amount paid, charged, or engaged to be paid, for purchasing goods, services and financial instruments.

label: Countermeasure
definition: An action taken against something undesirable or unwanted.

label: Course
definition: Passage through time; duration; a systematic or/and orderly succession; a sequence, e.g. course of treatment; a mode of action or behavior; natural development, typical manner of proceeding.

label: Crime
definition: An act that is punishable by law; illegal activities in general.

label: Criterion
definition: That which constitutes a standard from which a judgment can be established, a reference point against which other things can be evaluated, or a basis for comparison.

label: Cure
definition: Serving or tending to restore health; a remedy.

label: Curve
definition: A connected set of points constituting a one-dimensional continuum.

label: Damage
definition: Physical harm reducing the value, operation, or usefulness of something.

label: Data Element
definition: A unit of data for which the definition, identification, representation and permissible values are specified by means of a set of attributes.

label: Data Table
definition: A set of data arranged in rows and columns.

label: Debt
definition: Money that one person or entity owes or is required to pay to another person or entity.

label: Decision
definition: A position, opinion, or judgment reached on an issue under consideration.

label: Decompensation
definition: The inability of an organ or organ system to maintain homeostasis due to functional overload, often caused by the presence of disease intrinsic to the organ or organ system, or dysfunction in other organ systems.

label: Default
definition: Failure to meet an obligation.

label: Defect
definition: A failing or deficiency.

label: Defense
definition: Protection from harm.

label: Deficit
definition: A lack or deficiency.

label: Delinquency
definition: An act by a minor that is punishable by law.

label: Derivation Expression
definition: A character string containing a formal language expression that specifies how attributes are, should be, or have been derived from input parameters associated with activity.

label: Details
definition: Particulars considered individually and in relation to a whole.

label: Determine
definition: The establishment of a state or result.

label: Difficulty
definition: Something not easily done, accomplished, comprehended, or solved.

label: Discretion
definition: The freedom or authority to act according to one's own judgment.

label: Discrimination
definition: The unfair treatment of a person or group based on class or category bias.

label: Disposition
definition: The tendency of something to act in a certain manner under given circumstances resulting from natural constitution; nature; quality; orderly arrangement.

label: Dissent
definition: To hold or express an opinion that is counter to commonly or officially held opinions.

label: Disturbance
definition: A departure or divergence from that which is considered normal.

label: Domain
definition: An area of knowledge considered as a discrete unit for some purpose.

label: E-Z Notation
definition: A system for naming the stereoisomers of a molecule. The configuration at each double bond is determined by the priority of the side groups and whether the highest priority groups are on the same or opposite side. A Z indicates that the highest-priority side groups are on the same side and an E indicates that the highest-priority side groups are on opposite sides. Priority is given to the bonded atom with the higher atomic number.

label: Encase
definition: To completely and closely surround.

label: Entity
definition: An independently existing thing (living or nonliving).

label: Environment
definition: The totality of surrounding conditions.

label: Equation
definition: A statement written in symbols that two objects, e.g. mathematical expressions, or arrays of symbols representing a chemical reaction, are equal under certain conditions. Equations are usually and sometimes tacitly accompanied by the requirement to establish these conditions. In the case where mathematical or chemical objects contain arbitrary variables, such a requirement may mean finding those values of the variables that turn the equation into equality. Equations are sometimes called more explicitly as conditional equations.

label: Error
definition: A discrepancy between a computed, observed, or measured value or condition and the true, specified, or theoretically correct value or condition.

label: Event
definition: Something that happens at a given place and time.

label: Evidence
definition: Knowledge on which to base belief.

label: Example
definition: A representative form or pattern; an item of information that is representative of a type.

label: Exception
definition: An instance that does not conform to a rule or generalization.

label: Exemption
definition: Immunity from an obligation or duty.

label: Expectancy
definition: Something expected, especially the value (e.g. an amount, or length of time) or probability of occurrence for a specific event based on statistical calculations.

label: Experience
definition: The accumulation of knowledge or skill that results from direct participation in events or activities.

label: Expression
definition: A combination of symbols that represents a value.

label: Extent
definition: The range, magnitude, or distance over which a thing spans an interval of distance, space, or time.

label: Facility
definition: Services and space and equipment provided for a particular purpose; a building or place that provides a particular service or is used for a particular industry.

label: Factor
definition: Anything that contributes causally to a result.

label: Financial Account
definition: A formal relationship established to provide for regular banking and business services.

label: Fraction
definition: A part, a fragment of a whole.

label: Fragment
definition: A part of a larger entity.

label: Frame of Reference
definition: A set of ideas, as of philosophical or religious doctrine, in terms of which other ideas are interpreted or assigned meaning; a system that uses coordinates to establish position.

label: Functional Map
definition: A function such that for every element of one set there is a unique element of another set.

label: Funding
definition: A sum of money or other resources set aside for a specific purpose.

label: Geographic Area
definition: Any demarcated area of the Earth; may be determined by both natural and human boundaries.

label: Germ
definition: Something that may serve as the basis of further growth or development; an informal term for a bacterium.

label: Government
definition: The political organization by which a nation, stateor region is ruled.

label: Graph Edge
definition: A connection between nodes in a graph.

label: Graph Node
definition: One of the points that define a graph.

label: Group
definition: Any number of entities (members) considered as a unit.

label: Handle
definition: A number or token that lets a program access a resource. Programs often receive a handle in response to a request for a resource, and then they use the handle when they need to access the resource. When the program uses the handle, the value of the handle tells the system which resource, from the pool of resources maintained by the system, to use.

label: Have
definition: Used to describe the possession, relationships, or the state of something or someone.

label: Hazard
definition: A source of danger or risk.

label: History
definition: The aggregate of past events; the continuum of events occurring in succession leading from the past to the present; a record or narrative description of past events.

label: Hypogranularity
definition: A term that refers to the presence of diminished numbers of cytoplasmic granules in myeloid cells.

label: ICH Type Of Comparison
definition: Within the context of ICH, this data element describes how treatment arms will be compared within the clinical trial, to include trials that show superiority, non-equivalence, or non-inferiority, or dose-response relationships.

label: Incentive
definition: A benefit, consideration, or positive motivational influence, financial or otherwise, given to induce a specific action.

label: Increment
definition: The amount by which something increases.

label: Indication
definition: A sign, signal, circumstance, or symptom which serves to indicate or point out the cause, pathology, treatment, or issue of an attack of disease; the basis for initiation of a treatment for a disease or of a diagnostic test (causal, or symptomatic, or disease-specific indication).

label: Indicator
definition: An event, entity or condition that typically characterizes a prescribed environment or situation and determines or aids in determining whether certain stated circumstances exist or criteria are satisfied.

label: Information
definition: Knowledge derived from study, experience, or instruction that has been gathered or received by communication.

label: Infrastructure
definition: The basic facilities, equipment, or underlying framework that are necessary for a system or organization to function.

label: Ingredient
definition: That which enters into a compound, or is a component part of any combination or mixture; an element; a constituent.

label: Initials
definition: The first letter of a word, especially the first letter of each word in a proper name.

label: Initiator
definition: Something that sets into motion some course of events; a device that activates or releases or causes something to happen.

label: Input
definition: Anything added into a sytem.

label: Insecurity
definition: Uncertainty or anxiety that things are not fixed or safe.

label: Insert
definition: Something inserted or to be inserted.

label: Instance
definition: An occurrence of something.

label: Insurance and Payment Issues
definition: The effect of insurance coverage on medical care and practices.

label: Intellectual Property
definition: Creations of the mind, such as those created by musicians, authors, artists, and inventors.

label: Intent
definition: An anticipated outcome that is the purpose for actions taken, thus guiding the action.

label: International Normalized Ratio
definition: A system, commonly called the INR, established by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the International Committee on Thrombosis and Hemostasis for reporting the results of blood coagulation (clotting) tests. All results are standardized using the international sensitivity index (ISI) for the particular thromboplastin reagent and instrument combination utilized to perform the test; the ratio of a patient's clotting time to the lab's mean reference value is normalized against the ISI. (from medterms.com and medicine.ucsf.edu)

label: Interoperability
definition: The capability of two or more systems or components to use and transfer information reciprocally.

label: Intersection
definition: The set of elements common to two or more sets.

label: Intrusions
definition: Inclusions of words that were not on the test list.

label: Investigation Finding
definition: The result of an examination or inquiry.

label: Item Collection
definition: A group of items of the same type, gathered for display or study.

label: Job Decision Latitude
definition: The extent to which one can make decisions and exercise control over their work.

label: Jurisdiction
definition: The territory within which power can be exercised.

label: K Prime
definition: A dimensionless value that refers to the molar concentrations of chemical reactants and products under standard conditions.

label: Knowledge Field
definition: A branch of knowledge.

label: Lane
definition: A defined path with physical dimensions through which an object or substance may traverse.

label: Language
definition: A systematic means of communicating by the use of sounds, symbols, or gestures.

label: Layout
definition: A plan or design of something that is laid out.

label: Life
definition: The biochemical phenomenon that distinguishes organisms from inorganic matter. It is characterized by growth, metabolism, and reproduction.

label: Line of Response
definition: A line defined by a detector and its collimator in SPECT or by 2 coincident detectors in PET.

label: Lineage
definition: Descent in a line from a common progenitor or source.

label: Literacy
definition: The ability to read and write.

label: Loss
definition: A gradual decline in amount or activity.

label: Lot
definition: A defined quantity of manufactured material or product produced in a single manufacturing process or series of process steps so that it reasonably is expected to be homogeneous.

label: Material
definition: The tangible substance that goes into the makeup of a physical object.

label: Meal
definition: Any of the occasions for eating food that occur by custom or habit at more or less fixed times.

label: Meaning
definition: The idea or message that is intended, expressed, or signified.

label: Means
definition: A way of doing something; how a result is obtained or an end is achieved.

label: Mediator
definition: An agent that acts as a link between parties, objects, or actions.

label: Meridian
definition: A concept in Chinese medicine that holds that the pathways through which qi ("life force") flows are located throughout the body bilaterally, and exist beneath the surface of the skin.

label: Methodology
definition: The system of methods followed in a particular discipline.

label: Milestone
definition: An important event in the progress, development, or history of something or in someone's life.

label: Mismatch
definition: A failure to correspond or match; to match unsuitably or inaccurately.

label: Mode
definition: A particular functioning condition or arrangement.

label: Model
definition: A style or design of an item.

label: Model
definition: A representation of something, often idealized or modified to make it conceptually easier to understand.

label: Module
definition: A self-contained component (unit or item) that is used in combination with other components.

label: Movement
definition: A change in location or position.

label: Name
definition: The words or language units by which a thing is known.

label: Name and Version
definition: An identifier that includes the words or language units by which a thing is known and the form or variant of the original.

label: Need
definition: Anything that is necessary but lacking; the psychological feature that arouses an organism to action toward a desired goal; the reason for the action; that which gives purpose and direction to behavior.

label: Negation
definition: An operation in which a term denies or inverts the meaning of another term or construction.

label: Negligible
definition: Of such a small amount as to be considered insignificant.

label: Noise
definition: Any part of a signal that is not the true or original signal but is introduced by the communication mechanism.

label: Object
definition: Something perceptible by one or more of the senses, especially by vision or touch; a material thing.

label: Objective
definition: A goal or aim to be accomplished or attained.

label: Observer
definition: A person, organization, or device that makes an observation.

label: Occupation or Discipline
definition: A grouping of occupations and fields of study.

label: Occupational Prestige
definition: The differential social worth which is ascribed to jobs or occupations.

label: Odor
definition: The sensation that results when olfactory receptors in the nose are stimulated by particular chemicals in gaseous form.

label: Opening
definition: An aperture or hole leading into something, especially a bodily cavity.

label: Operator
definition: A symbol used to perform an arithmetic or logical operation.

label: Options
definition: Alternative choices or actions.

label: Origin
definition: The place where something begins or comes from in either the sense of a physical location or a metaphysical source.

label: Outcome
definition: The result of an action.

label: Outlier
definition: An observation in a data set that is numerically distant from the rest of the data.

label: Output
definition: Anything coming out of a system.

label: Overlap
definition: The phenomenon of coinciding partially or wholly; extending over and covering a part of.

label: Ownership
definition: The legal relation in which a person or group entity has a financial interest in, dominion of, or control over an object or property.

label: Part
definition: One of the portions into which something is regarded as divided and which together constitute a whole.

label: Participate
definition: To take part in an activity.

label: Party
definition: An informal term specifying an organization or person.

label: Passage
definition: A path through which water or other materials may flow or travel.

label: Password
definition: A secret set of characters, numbers, or symbols used as a form of authentication.

label: Patient Base
definition: The total number of patients that have been evaluated or treated.

label: Patient Load
definition: The number of patients who are evaluated or treated in any given period of time.

label: Patient Reported Outcome Stem
definition: The prompt, question or instruction in a patient-reported outcome item. (CDISC Glossary)

label: Patient-Reported Outcome Item Generation
definition: The process for identifying the individual questions, statements, or tasks within a patient reported outcome (PRO) instrument.

label: Patient-reported Outcome Item
definition: A question, statement, or task and its pre-specified response values within a patient-reported outcome instrument.

label: Pattern
definition: An arrangement of objects, facts, behaviors, or other things which have scientific, mathematical, geometric, statistical, or other meaning.

label: Pay
definition: Money or other benefits received in exchange for work.

label: Peak
definition: The most extreme possible amount or value; the highest point.

label: Performance
definition: A manner of functioning or operating.

label: Perseverations
definition: Repeats of words that have been previously mentioned on the same trial.

label: Person
definition: A human being.

label: Phase
definition: A distinguishable part, a stage in a series of events or in a process of development, e.g. any of the varying aspects or stages in course of a disease; a fraction of a cycle.

label: Phenomenon
definition: A fact or situation that is observed to exist.

label: Phoneme
definition: A speech sound that is distinguished by the speakers of a particular language.

label: Picture
definition: A visual representation of an object, scene, person or abstraction.

label: Pixel
definition: The smallest resolvable rectangular area of an image, either on a screen or stored in memory.

label: Place
definition: The particular portion of space occupied by a physical object or intended for a physical object.

label: Plane
definition: An imaginary surface formed by extension of a point through any axis or two definite points; any flat two-dimensional surface.

label: Plug
definition: An object designed to fill a hole tightly; anything that resembles such an object in appearance or origin.

label: Point
definition: The precise location of something; a spatially limited location.

label: Pointer
definition: An address or abstract location where a specified object can be found.

label: Potential
definition: The likelihood or possibility that a specific state or condition will exist or that an action will occur.

label: Precaution
definition: A measure taken beforehand to prevent harm or secure good.

label: Precursor
definition: A substance from which another substance is formed.

label: Prediction
definition: An extrapolation of given data into the future.

label: Preference
definition: The act, fact, or principle of giving advantages to some particular person, object, or course of action over others; designated as more desirable.

label: Prepuberty
definition: The period of development immediately prior to the onset of puberty when changes occur that lead to sexual maturity.

label: Problem
definition: A difficulty, disorder, or condition needing resolution.

label: Process
definition: A sequence of events which produces some outcome.

label: Program
definition: A broad framework of goals to be achieved.

label: Progress
definition: The act of moving forward toward a goal.

label: Project
definition: Any specifically defined piece of work that is undertaken or attempted to meet a single requirement.

label: Projection
definition: A prediction made by extrapolating from past observations.

label: Property
definition: Any tangible or intangible possession that is owned by a person, group, or organization.

label: Provenance
definition: Where something originated or was nurtured in its early existence.

label: Provider
definition: One that makes something available.

label: Psychological Demand
definition: The mental arousal or stimulation necessary to accomplish a task.

label: Puberty
definition: The stage in life when sex glands become functional. It is characterized by the development of secondary sex characteristics, reproductive functions, and growth spurts.

label: Purpose
definition: What something is used for; reason or intention.

label: Qualifying
definition: Success in satisfying a test or requirement.

label: Quotient
definition: A quantity obtained by division.

label: Randomization Weight
definition: The relative proportion of subjects to be randomized.
